methodology_summary: Winners are selected on a transparent rubric: vertical fit score (40%), aggregate user rating from G2 + Capterra (25%), pricing transparency (15%), feature depth (10%), and editorial fit for the buyer profile (10%). No vendor paid for placement. Where WrenchStack has an affiliate relationship with a winner, that's disclosed on the tool's review page. Recommendations are unchanged whether the affiliate is active or not.

categories.label: Best Overall FSM 2026
categories.why: Jobber wins overall on the combination buyers actually optimize for: 4.6/5 aggregate rating across 4,200+ reviews, mid-tier pricing that scales from solo operators to ~50-user shops, broad vertical coverage (HVAC, plumbing, electrical, landscaping, cleaning, roofing, GC), and a mobile app that field techs actually open without complaining. It's the platform that fits the most buyers without major caveats.
categories.buyer_signal: Best for SMB contractors (2-25 users) who serve residential customers and need balanced feature depth without enterprise complexity.

categories.label: Best Enterprise FSM 2026
categories.why: ServiceTitan is the enterprise standard for residential trades — HVAC, plumbing, electrical operators above $5M revenue routinely consolidate on it. The reasons are unglamorous but real: deep dispatching automation, multi-location support, pricebook integration, and call-center features no SMB platform attempts. Custom-quoted pricing ($300+/user/mo typical) and 3-6 month implementation are the cost.
categories.buyer_signal: Best for established residential trades shops ($1M+ revenue, 10+ techs) where the cost of inefficiency now exceeds the cost of enterprise software.

categories.label: Best for HVAC 2026
categories.why: Sera Systems wins the HVAC mid-market — the segment between Jobber (too generalist) and ServiceTitan (too expensive). Built specifically for residential HVAC with maintenance agreement automation, IAQ accessory selling, and equipment-installation workflows. Mid-tier pricing ($169-249/user/mo) accessible to shops doing $750K-$5M.
categories.buyer_signal: Best for residential HVAC shops doing $750K-$5M revenue with 5-25 techs who want vertical-specific workflows without enterprise pricing.

categories.label: Best for Plumbing 2026
categories.why: Workiz wins plumbing on the SMB-to-mid-market range. Strong scheduling + dispatching for service-call-heavy businesses, native two-way SMS that plumbers actually use, accessible pricing ($165-329/mo), and high vertical-fit scoring (9/10) for plumbing-specific workflows like emergency dispatch and one-call-resolution.
categories.buyer_signal: Best for plumbing shops (2-20 users) where dispatch speed and SMS communication are the daily bottleneck.

categories.label: Best for Electrical 2026
categories.why: Housecall Pro wins electrical for the strong combination of mobile-first field-tech UX, customer-facing communications (text confirmations, on-my-way tracking, digital payments), and electrical-shop-friendly pricing ($59-279/mo per user). Tech adoption is the hidden bottleneck in electrical and HCP's mobile app gets installed without resistance.
categories.buyer_signal: Best for electrical contractors who care about field-tech adoption and customer-facing professionalism more than back-office accounting depth.

categories.label: Best for Roofing 2026
categories.why: AccuLynx wins roofing because it's the only mature platform built specifically for roofers — insurance/restoration claims workflow, aerial measurement integrations (EagleView/Hover), supplier integrations (ABC/Beacon/SRS), and crew management built around the roofing job cycle. Pricing is custom-quoted but lands in the mid-tier range for most roofing shops.
categories.buyer_signal: Best for established roofing contractors ($2M+ revenue) doing insurance/storm work who need claims-workflow depth.

categories.label: Best for SMB Roofing 2026
categories.why: Roofr wins SMB roofing on price + ease. $49-149/mo entry-tier pricing, integrated aerial reports, fast quote-to-contract workflow, and a UX modern enough that solo-to-3-crew roofers can run the whole job from quote to invoice without back-office staff. Beats AccuLynx for shops too small to justify enterprise complexity.
categories.buyer_signal: Best for solo and 2-3 crew roofers who need quote-to-job-to-invoice without enterprise overhead.

categories.label: Best for Landscaping 2026
categories.why: Aspire wins landscaping with vertical-specific design that generalists miss: per-property job histories, crew route optimization, materials/labor estimating built around landscape-job math, seasonal-contract billing, and recurring-maintenance contract workflows. Custom-quoted pricing lands accessibly for established commercial landscape shops, and the platform handles the install-side complexity (design-build) as well as recurring maintenance.
categories.buyer_signal: Best for established landscape contractors (5-30 users) doing recurring maintenance contracts plus install/design projects.

categories.label: Best for Cleaning 2026
categories.why: ZenMaid wins cleaning by being built only for cleaning. Booking + recurring scheduling tuned to maid-service rhythms, customer-portal that handles the cleaning-specific reschedule patterns, automated reminders, and pricing accessible to solo cleaners ($58/mo entry). Generalist FSM platforms have features cleaning shops don't need but pay for.
categories.buyer_signal: Best for residential cleaning businesses (solo to 20 cleaners) who want vertical-specific workflows without paying for unused features.

categories.label: Best for Pest Control 2026
categories.why: FieldRoutes wins pest control on modern UX + route optimization. Built specifically for pest companies with recurring service plans, automated billing on recurring frequency, route density optimization (a pest-route-specific math problem), and customer-portal features that match pest industry norms. Modern alternative to legacy PestPac.
categories.buyer_signal: Best for pest control operators (3-50 users) who want modern UI + route density optimization.

categories.label: Best for Pool Service 2026
categories.why: Skimmer wins pool service by being built only for pool routes. Chemical readings, water-test logging, equipment-installation tracking, photo documentation, and route optimization tuned to pool-service density. Generalist FSM tools don't even attempt pool-specific workflow depth.
categories.buyer_signal: Best for pool service businesses (solo to 25 techs) who want pool-specific workflow depth — chemical logging, water tests, equipment tracking.

categories.label: Best for Solo Operators / 1-3 Users 2026
categories.why: Joist wins solo operators on price + simplicity. Free for basics (quoting, invoicing), $14-26/mo for full features. Designed for the 1-3-user shop that doesn't need dispatching, multi-tech routing, or back-office staff. Mobile-first because solo contractors live in their truck.
categories.buyer_signal: Best for solo contractors and 2-3-person operations doing residential service work without dispatch complexity.

categories.label: Best Value Entry-Tier 2026
categories.why: Service Fusion wins entry-tier value with flat-rate unlimited-user pricing ($149-249/mo total, not per-user). For shops with 5+ users this beats every per-user platform by a factor of 2-4x at scale. Feature depth matches mid-tier per-user platforms — full FSM stack including dispatching, invoicing, recurring jobs.
categories.buyer_signal: Best for cost-conscious shops with 5-20 users where per-user pricing math gets painful.

categories.label: Best Mobile UX 2026
categories.why: Housecall Pro wins mobile UX because it was built mobile-first when most FSM tools were still bolting iOS onto desktop-era platforms. Field techs install it without resistance, customers get text-confirmations that actually work, and on-my-way tracking is a real feature, not a marketing claim. Mobile app store ratings 4.7+/5 across iOS and Android.
categories.buyer_signal: Best for shops where field-tech adoption is the bottleneck and mobile-app polish matters more than back-office feature depth.

categories.label: Best Free Tier 2026
categories.why: Joist's free tier is genuinely usable — quotes, invoices, payment collection, no row-count limits. Most 'free' FSM tools are crippled demos; Joist runs full quoting and invoicing free and only charges for advanced features. Solo contractors can run their entire business on free for 6+ months before needing paid upgrade.
categories.buyer_signal: Best for solo contractors and bootstrappers who can't justify any monthly cost until revenue scales.

categories.label: Best CMMS 2026
categories.why: MaintainX wins CMMS on modern UX + mobile-first workflow at a price point legacy CMMS systems can't match. Preventive maintenance scheduling, work order management, asset tracking, and parts inventory all in a mobile-first interface that maintenance techs adopt without IT-led training. 4.8/4.8 G2/Capterra ratings — highest in the CMMS category.
categories.buyer_signal: Best for facilities/maintenance teams (10-100 users) modernizing from paper/whiteboard or aging CMMS platforms.

categories.label: Best Residential Construction PM 2026
categories.why: Buildertrend wins residential construction PM with the deepest workflow depth for custom-home builders and large remodelers — change order management, AIA-style billing, customer-facing portal that handles construction-specific communications (selections, schedule updates, photo logs), and pricing accessible to 5-50-employee construction shops.
categories.buyer_signal: Best for residential builders and large remodelers (3-30 projects/year, $1M-$20M revenue) managing complex jobs with selections, change orders, and AIA billing.

categories.label: Best Customer Support 2026
categories.why: Jobber wins customer support on consistency: phone + chat + email support across all tiers (not just enterprise), an active community forum where staff respond, video training library that doesn't gate the basics behind sales, and a track record across 4,200+ Capterra reviews where support quality is mentioned as a positive 3x more often than negative.
categories.buyer_signal: Best for shops without IT staff who need responsive support during ramp-up and ongoing operation.
